data.title: Astronova expects mid-single-digit fiscal 2027 revenue growth as $2M annualized royalty benefit begins in Q4
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AstroNova, Inc. (ALOT) Q4 fiscal 2026

Management view

  • "The second half of fiscal 2026 was a reset period for AstroNova, and our results reflect the early benefits of the changes we have made across the business." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • "We entered the year with a focus on stabilizing the company, improving cash generation, reducing debt and raising accountability across both segments, and we delivered against those priorities." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • "As you know, we announced that the Board is evaluating a range of potential strategic alternatives, which may include, among other things, a sale of all or part of the company, a strategic investment, a merger or other business combination, other strategic or financial options or continuing to execute on our organic strategic plan." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann) "We are early in the process, and as you would expect, we cannot speculate on the outcome." (President, CEO & Director Ittmann)
  • "Product ID orders were $27.5 million, up $2.9 million year-over-year, resulting in a book-to-bill ratio of 104% and backlog increased by $1.1 million sequentially as our new go-to-market strategy continued to gain traction." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • "Orders in Aerospace were $13.6 million, resulting in a book-to-bill ratio of 122% and year-end backlog was $12 million." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann) "ToughWriter now represents more than 80% of total flight deck printers shipments." (President, CEO & Director Ittmann)
  • "Fourth quarter revenue was $37.5 million, up $0.2 million compared with the prior year period." (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle) "Tariff mitigation actions contributed approximately $0.6 million to revenue in the quarter, and the foreign currency translation provided a $0.8 million benefit." (VP, Treasurer & CFO DeByle)

Outlook

  • "Looking ahead, for fiscal 2027, we expect mid-single-digit revenue growth and expansion in adjusted EBITDA margin." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • "In Aerospace, we anticipated measured top line growth supported by rising aircraft utilization and favorable shift in product mix and the expiration of a major royalty obligation in the third quarter of fiscal 2027, which will provide an approximate $2 million annualized contribution to gross profit beginning in the fourth quarter." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s full-year framing, Q4 shifted to a fiscal 2027 setup and emphasized that "we have spent the last 6 months positioning AstroNova for improved and more sustainable performance" (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann), alongside the strategic alternatives review.

Financial results

  • "Net loss for the quarter was $1.1 million or $0.15 per diluted share." (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle) "Non-GAAP net loss was $0.3 million or $0.04 per share." (VP, Treasurer & CFO DeByle)
  • "Gross profit for the fourth quarter was $11.3 million and gross margin was 30.2%, reflecting a contraction of 250 to 260 basis points year-over-year, primarily to lower volume and mix." (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle) "On a non-GAAP basis, gross profit was $11.9 million and non-GAAP gross margin was 31.7%." (VP, Treasurer & CFO DeByle)
  • "Adjusted EBITDA in the fourth quarter grew 18% to $3.3 million, while adjusted EBITDA margin expanded 130 basis points to 8.8%." (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle) "For the full fiscal year 2026, adjusted EBITDA was $12.7 million." (VP, Treasurer & CFO DeByle)
  • "Cash provided by operating activities in the fourth quarter was $3.7 million" and "cash from operations was $11.7 million" for the full year. (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle) "Capital expenditures were tightly controlled at $0.3 million for the year." (VP, Treasurer & CFO DeByle)
  • "During the fourth quarter, we reduced debt by $2.7 million, bringing total debt to $37.6 million." (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle) "We ended the year with $4.1 million of cash and cash equivalents and total liquidity of $15.9 million." (VP, Treasurer & CFO DeByle)

Q&A

  • No analyst Q&A session was included in this transcript ("There are no questions at this time."). (Operator)

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts: No questions were asked in the current quarter, limiting direct read-through on analyst skepticism or focus areas.
  • Management: Slightly positive in tone, anchored in execution claims and forward-looking margin drivers, including "we delivered against those priorities" and "we expect mid-single-digit revenue growth." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • Versus the prior quarter: Both calls had no questions, while Q4 introduced a clearer potential catalyst and uncertainty with "the Board is evaluating a range of potential strategic alternatives." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Strategic focus: Q4 added a board-led strategic alternatives process; Q3 centered on an operating transformation narrative and reiterated fiscal 2026 guidance.
  • Guidance language: Q3 said, "We are reiterating our guidance for the full year of fiscal 2026" and provided a revenue range and adjusted EBITDA margin range; Q4 moved to fiscal 2027 targets of "mid-single-digit revenue growth" and "expansion in adjusted EBITDA margin." (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • Key business drivers: Both quarters emphasized ToughWriter mix at "more than 80%" of flight deck shipments and a fiscal 2027 royalty roll-off; Q4 quantified the roll-off as "approximately a $2 million annualized benefit to gross profit" beginning in fiscal Q4 2027. (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)

Risks and concerns

  • "We are early in the process, and as you would expect, we cannot speculate on the outcome" of strategic alternatives, leaving timing and result unspecified. (President, CEO & Director Jorik Ittmann)
  • "Gross margin" faced pressure "primarily to lower volume and mix" in Q4, with management positioning second-half comparisons as the better indicator of trajectory. (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle)
  • "Quarter-to-quarter order patterns do not necessarily reflect underlying demand" given Aerospace project timing, which management highlighted as a recurring variability factor. (VP, Treasurer & CFO Thomas DeByle)

Final takeaway

Management framed fiscal 2026 as a second-half “reset,” pointed to improving orders and backlog visibility, and guided to mid-single-digit fiscal 2027 revenue growth with adjusted EBITDA margin expansion. The call elevated two stock-relevant swing factors: the board’s active review of strategic alternatives and a royalty expiration expected to contribute about $2 million annualized to gross profit beginning in fiscal Q4 2027, while the absence of analyst questions left limited external probing of execution and timing.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BNP Paribas starts coverage of HVAC sector: Near-term chill amid long-term growth
data.description:
The U.S. HVAC industry is entering a period of mixed performance, with strong long-term growth drivers offset by near-term weakness in residential markets, according to a new research report from BNP Paribas.

BNP Paribas U.S. HVAC  Coverage
       
Company (ticker) Rating
Trane Technologies (TT) Outperform
Vertiv (VRT) Outperform
Lennox International (LII) Neutral
Carrier Global (CARR) Neutral
Johnson Controls (JCI)  Underperform

The sector, which generates about $230 billion in annual global revenue, is being reshaped by structural forces such as electrification, energy efficiency mandates, digitization and the rapid expansion of AI-driven data centers. These trends are expected to support above-average growth over time, even as short-term conditions remain uneven.

Data centers drive next growth phase

Analysts led by Andrew Buscaglia highlight data centers as the most important driver of incremental demand, particularly as artificial intelligence workloads increase cooling requirements.

Companies focused on thermal management and liquid cooling are positioned to benefit most. Vertiv (VRT), which derives more than 80% of its revenue from data centers, is seen as a direct beneficiary of rising demand for high-density cooling systems.

Trane Technologies (TT) is also expected to gain share, with analysts pointing to its pricing power and exposure to commercial HVAC markets. Both companies were initiated with positive ratings.

“Data centers will drive above-average growth in HVAC this cycle,” the report said, with demand for advanced cooling solutions accelerating as AI infrastructure expands.

Residential market remains a drag

In contrast, residential HVAC continues to face headwinds. Higher interest rates and housing affordability challenges are limiting new construction and slowing demand for new systems.

The report expects weakness in residential markets to persist through at least 2026, with a more meaningful recovery unlikely until 2027.

Recent regulatory changes tied to refrigerant transitions have also disrupted volumes, though those effects are beginning to ease. Even so, analysts warn that pricing fatigue among consumers could weigh on demand in the near term.

Commercial outlook mixed outside of AI

Outside of data centers, commercial HVAC demand remains uneven.

Segments such as healthcare and education are showing resilience, while office and retail markets continue to face uncertainty. Manufacturing demand is expected to improve following delayed capital spending.

Overall, analysts describe the commercial outlook as mixed, with data centers standing out as the clearest source of growth.

Stock picks reflect diverging exposure

BNP Paribas initiated coverage on five HVAC-related companies with varying ratings based on their exposure to these trends.

  • Trane Technologies (TT) and Vertiv (VRT) received positive ratings
  • Carrier Global (CARR) and Lennox International (LII) were rated neutral
  • Johnson Controls (JCI) was assigned a negative rating

The firm cited structural advantages and data center exposure as key differentiators, while warning that companies tied more heavily to residential markets face ongoing pressure.

Valuations reflect AI optimism

Valuations across the sector have risen in recent quarters, driven in part by investor enthusiasm for AI infrastructure.

Data center–focused companies command higher multiples, while traditional HVAC names trade at a discount, especially those with greater residential exposure.

Despite elevated valuations, analysts note that the sector is now trading slightly below the broader industrials index, which could present opportunities if fundamentals improve.

Long-term outlook remains intact

Over the longer term, the report remains constructive on HVAC, citing durable demand tied to energy efficiency and climate trends.

Buildings account for roughly 40% of global energy consumption, and HVAC systems play a central role in reducing emissions and improving efficiency.

At the same time, only about 10% of people living in the world’s hottest climates have access to air conditioning, underscoring the potential for future growth.

While near-term conditions may remain uneven, analysts conclude that structural drivers are likely to support sustained expansion across the industry over the coming decade.

data.title: GameStop to launch its digital trading card platform on April 15
data.description:
GameStop Corp. (GME) announced on Tuesday that its digital trading card platform is launching to the public on April 15.

The retailer described Power Packs as an online experience where collectors can purchase digital packs to unlock real, PSA-graded trading cards. Every card is securely stored in the PSA Vault and can be sold back instantly, shipped home, or added to a customer’s collection. Pokémon, football, basketball, and baseball categories are available at launch, with packs starting at $25 and ranging up to $2,500.

The way it works is that a Power Packs customer pays for a pack, opens it digitally, and receives a PSA-graded trading card tied to that pack. The physical card stays stored in PSA Vault, and the customer can later ship it to itself, instantly sell it back to GameStop (GME), or potentially sell it through PSA’s eBay integration.

For investors, Power Packs is GameStop's (GME) attempt to turn card collecting into a more platform-like experience. It is unclear if the business will align directly with GameStop's (GME) M&A ambitions.

Shares of GameStop (GME) were up 1.3% in Tuesday morning trading to $23.70 vs. the 52-week range of $19.93 to $35.81. Short interest on GME stands at 14.0% of the total float.

data.title: Janus Living initiated with Buy at BofA, KeyBanc Capital
data.description:
Janus Living (JAN) was initiated with a Buy rating at BofA Securities and a Buy-equivalent (Overweight rating) at KeyBanc Capital Markets.

Shares were 2.58% higher to $24.61 after the market opened on Tuesday.

"Externally managed by Healthpeak Properties (DOC), JAN is a pure-play senior housing REIT with 100% NOI exposure to high-quality, RIDEA-structured senior housing operating platforms and continuing care retirement communities," said BoA in a research note.

"We believe JAN's senior housing-focused strategy, conservative balance sheet, and strong operator base position the company to deliver above-average earnings growth over time," added the note, with a $29 price objective.

Meanwhile, KeyBanc Capital Markets set a price target of $28 on the stock.

"The portfolio currently consists of 34 senior housing communities (~10,400 units) across 10 states, including 15 Life Plan/CCRCs and 19 SHOP  communities," said KeyBanc analysts.

"JAN's internal growth outlook in the low-double-digit range is further supported by the company's pristine balance sheet, which has no outstanding debt and approximately $1.4B of available liquidity post-IPO," said the research note.

"With a robust investment landscape, JAN's net cash position is a key differentiator for the company to fully deploy IPO proceeds within its first year as a public company at attractive yields in the 6-7% range. Given JAN's unique pure-play SHOP exposure, favorable senior housing operating fundamentals, and embedded external growth from a well-defined acquisition pipeline, we view valuation as offering an attractive entry point relative to its healthcare REIT peers," said the note.

Notably, Janus Living is rated as Hold by Seeking Alpha authors.

KeyBanc established a normalized FFO estimate of $0.96 per share for 2026 and $1.13 per share for 2027. The consensus stands at $0.67 and $0.86, respectively.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: BE, WFC, GSAT, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched up Tuesday while oil slid back below $100/bbl as investors eyed signs of potential progress in U.S.-Iran negotiations.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Bloom Energy (BE) +15% - Shares surged after Oracle ((ORCL)) expanded its partnership with the firm to procure up to 2.8 GW of fuel cell capacity to power AI data centers, with an initial 1.2 GW already contracted and deployment underway through 2027. The deal underscores accelerating demand for reliable, fast-deploying energy solutions to support AI infrastructure, positioning Bloom’s modular fuel cells as a key enabler of rapid data center buildouts.
  • Globalstar (GSAT) +10% - Shares climbed after Amazon.com (AMZN) agreed to acquire the satellite operator in a deal valued at ~$11.5B, strengthening its low-Earth orbit “Leo” network and enabling direct-to-device connectivity. The transaction offers GSAT shareholders $90 per share in cash or stock (capped) and is expected to close in 2027. Amazon also expanded its ecosystem by partnering with Apple ((AAPL)) to integrate satellite services into iPhones and Apple Watches, allowing users to access emergency, messaging, and location features beyond cellular coverage, positioning Amazon to better compete with SpaceX’s Starlink network. Amazon (AMZN) shares up 3% following the announcement.
  • Credo Technology (CRDO) +14% - Shares jumped after announcing it will acquire DustPhotonics in a cash-and-stock deal valued at $750M upfront plus potential earnouts, strengthening its position in high-speed optical connectivity. The acquisition of silicon photonics (SiPho PIC) capabilities is expected to enhance Credo’s optical transceiver portfolio amid rising AI-driven data center demand, with the deal projected to be accretive to non-GAAP EPS by FY2027.
  • BlackRock (BLK) +4% – Shares rose after Q1 results topped expectations, driven by strong 8% Y/Y organic fee growth and margin expansion, with adjusted EPS of $12.53 and revenue of $6.7B, both beating estimates. While long-term inflows of $136B came in below consensus and slowed sequentially, record iShares ETF inflows of $132B and continued strength in private markets helped offset softer momentum, even as assets under management dipped slightly Q/Q to $13.89T.

Biggest stock losers

  • Allogene Therapeutics (ALLO) -13% - Shares fell after announcing a $175M underwritten public offering of common stock, with an additional ~$26M greenshoe option. The company said proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including funding clinical trials, R&D, and operational expenses.
  • CarMax (KMX) -12% – Shares slid despite FQ4 earnings beat, as weak demand trends weighed on sentiment, with retail used unit sales down 0.8% and comparable sales declining 1.9%. Revenue slipped due to lower volumes and softer pricing, while total gross profit dropped 9.4% amid declining margins across both retail and wholesale segments, highlighting ongoing pressure on profitability even as the company continues to invest in expansion and long-term growth initiatives.
  • Wells Fargo (WFC) -5% – Shares fell after mixed Q1 results, as net interest income of $12.1B missed expectations and net interest margin compressed to 2.47%, raising concerns about ongoing margin pressure despite a higher-rate environment. While EPS of $1.60 beat estimates and revenue rose 6.5% Y/Y to $21.45B, the report was weighed down by softer NII trends and a 22% jump in provisions for credit losses, signaling cautious credit conditions even as core business segments showed steady growth.

data.title: GoPro launches new MISSION camera series
data.description:
GoPro (GPRO) launched a new series of cameras featuring the smallest, lightest, and most rugged equipment with an “ultra-efficient” GP3 processor, designed for “cinematic versatility in the most demanding environments.”

“With the launch of the MISSION 1 Series, GoPro is entering the premium end of the digital market in a significant way,” said GoPro’s senior vice president of product, Pablo Lema.

The combination of a 50-megapixel 1-inch sensor and GP3 processor allows for high performance in a compact camera.

The MISSION 1 PRO ILS and MISSION 1 include an accessory ecosystem with a new wireless mic system, point-and-shoot and powered grip, higher capacity Enduro 2 battery, and I/O expansion media mod.

Pricing for the new products will be revealed at the NAB tradeshow, April 19-22.

Fueled by Monday’s announcement to explore additional opportunities in the defense and space industries, GoPro (GPRO) shares are higher for a second consecutive day with a gain of more than 5% at Tuesday’s open.

data.title: Viking Holdings trades higher after Rothschild points to long-term upside
data.description:
Viking Holdings Ltd. (VIK) moved higher in early trading on Tuesday after Rothschild & Co. Redburn upgraded the cruise line stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Neutral.

Analyst Alex Brignall and his team see further upside for the stock despite the near-term concerns over fuel prices. The analyst team noted that on a normalized free cash flow basis, Viking's (VIK) multiple is close to those of Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) and Carnival (CCL) and at a significant discount to that of Royal Caribbean (RCL).

Looking ahead, Rothschild sees Viking (VIK) benefitting from "healthy secular demand drivers, best-in-class revenue visibility, and strong cash generation."

Shares of Viking (VIK) were up 2.2% in mid-morning trading. The cruise line stock has a consensus Buy rating with both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts.

data.title: Credo surges after deal to acquire photonics company
data.description:
Shares of Credo Technology (CRDO) surged about 13% on Tuesday after the company announced postmarket on Monday that it is acquiring DustPhotonics in a cash and stock deal.

DustPhotonics develops Silicon Photonics Photonic Integrated Circuit, or SiPho PIC, technology for optical transceivers.

Credo will pay $750M cash upfront and about 0.92M common shares. The company may pay incremental contingent consideration of up to about 3.21M shares based on achieving certain milestones.

Credo said the acquisition accelerates its optical interconnect roadmap and significantly expands its served addressable market in the global optical industry. These SiPho PICs are deployed in transceivers at leading hyperscale AI clusters and are also in design for leading Near Port Optics ((NPO)) and Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) applications.

The company noted DustPhotonics' SiPho PICs are deployed in transceivers at leading hyperscale AI clusters and are also in design for leading Near Port Optics, or NPO, and Co-Packaged Optics, or CPO, applications. 

"This acquisition is encouraging as it supports vertical integration of Credo’s optical transceiver offering. Additionally, we believe this acquisition enables Credo to broaden its scale-up networking opportunity to SiPh-based CPO/NPO solutions. Because we already forecast in FY27 that Credo will generate $200M in optical DSP [Digital Signal Processors] sales and $100M in ZF optical transceiver sales, we think Credo’s outlook implies that DustPhotonics could generate at least $200M in sales in FY27. Without considering revenue and expenses, DustPhotonics will need to generate at least ~$20M in net income to be accretive to Credo’s FY27 non-GAAP earnings," said BNP Paribas' analysts David O’Connor and Karl Ackerman.

Related stocks: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) slumped about 4%, while Astera Labs (ALAB) and Ciena (CIEN) each fell nearly 3%. Lumentum (LITE) declined around 2%, while Coherent (COHR) dipped about 1%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) seemed to buck the trend and climbed about 2% on Tuesday.

Update: The story was updated with a BNP note and stock price movement.

data.title: S&P 500 returns to positive territory and erases Middle East conflict losses
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) has staged a strong comeback, returning to positive territory for the year and fully recovering losses tied to the recent U.S.-Iran conflict in the Middle East. The benchmark index is now trading at 6,922, up 1.5% on the session and 0.6% higher year to date.

This marks a notable turnaround from its recent low of 6,317.41, recorded on March 30, from which the index has rallied roughly 9.5%. The recovery underscores renewed investor confidence and resilient market momentum despite geopolitical uncertainties.

Importantly, the S&P 500 now sits just 1% below its all-time high of just over 7,000, which it reached on January 28, signaling that equities are approaching record levels once again. 

The sharp rebound has been driven by strength across key sectors, with top-performing stocks leading the advance and helping fuel the broader market’s recovery.

Outlined below are the top 20 S&P 500 performing stocks of 2026: 

  1. Sandisk (SNDK), +295.5% YTD
  2. Lumentum (LITE), +138.75% YTD
  3. Ciena (CIEN), +105.74% YTD 
  4. Western Digital (WDC), +101.49% YTD
  5. Corning (GLW), +95.09% YTD
  6. Teradyne (TER), +88.97% YTD
  7. Vertiv (VRT), +86.70% YTD 
  8. Seagate (STX), +84.70% YTD

  10. Moderna (MRNA), +73.60% YTD
  11. Intel (INTC), +71.30% YTD 
  12. LyondellBasell (LYB), +68.12% YTD
  13. Coherent (COHR), +67.54% YTD 
  14. Dow Inc. (DOW), +64.39% YTD

  16. Keysight (KEYS), +62.31% YTD
  17. Lam Research (LRCX), +57.96% YTD
  18. Applied Materials (AMAT), +54.78% YTD
  19. APA Corp (APA), +54.66% YTD 
  20. CF Industries (CF), +51.40% YTD

S&P 500 Funds: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Omega Healthcare cut to Sell-equivalent at BoA Securities
data.description:
Omega Healthcare Investors (OHI) was trading lower as BoA Securities cut its recommendation on the stock amid competition and regulatory risks.

The stock was downgraded to Underperform from Neutral, with the price target reduced to $46 from $52. OHI was 0.81% down to $45.05 Tuesday pre-market.

"With total return potential of 7.2%, OHI screens as an Underperform within our U.S. REIT coverage. Reflecting heightened competitive dynamics, regulatory pressures, and asset quality considerations relative to peers, we are reducing our FY26 AFFO estimate in line with the Street and lowering our AFFO multiple to 15.0x from 16.5x," said analysts Farrell Granath, Jeffrey Spector, and Julieta Michelin.

The research note said an upside to earnings is contingent on accretive external growth execution and continued improvement in tenant credit quality.

"As WELL, SBRA, and CTRE remain active SNF (skilled nursing facility) acquirers, OHI appears relatively disadvantaged in a more competitive market. Its larger market cap requires materially sized deals to impact earnings at increasingly competitive prices," said BofA analysts.

"Thus, we are cutting our FY26 acquisition volume assumption to $633M from $929M, reducing AFFO/share estimate ~80 basis points," said the note.

"While the subsector (SNF) benefits from favorable demographic tailwinds, it faces elevated regulatory and public reimbursement risk," said the note.

"Proposed tighter regulation, potential limits on REIT ownership, and a lower FY27 SNF PPS initial update of +2.4% (from +3.2% in FY26) could pressure tenant coverage despite stable fundamentals resulting in downward pressure on multiples," said the analysts.

BoA's rating differs from the sell-side analysts, Seeking Alpha authors, and Quant ratings of Buy.
